_about: Candidate forced-both lexical items for the discriminating test named by essay/layer-specialness-vs-always-resolvability, built under the resolved gate decisions/resolved/forced-both-lexical-operationalization (Q1-iii zeugma/co-predication frame + independent balance check; Q2-i forced-single-application instrument). These are AUTHOR-BUILT co-predication frames over HOMONYMS (unrelated senses), each yoking one token to two complements that demand its two senses together. NOT FROZEN: this is a build-attempt record; the items did not clear Q1-ii certification (see README.md). No model was ever queried with them.
frame: co-predication / zeugma: one token of the homonym, two complements that each select a different unrelated sense, so a single-sense reading makes one complement anomalous.

items.copredication: The bank was slick with mud and badly short on cash.
items.single_sense_anomaly: Read 'river-edge' only -> 'short on cash' is anomalous; read 'finance' only -> 'slick with mud' is anomalous.
items.forced_single_application_q2i: To proceed you must treat the bank as a physical place you can stand on. Can a person walk along the bank's edge? Answer YES or NO and give the one reason. (Only the river reading licenses an answer; 'it means both' cannot complete the task.)

items.copredication: The club was oak-hard and strictly members-only.
items.single_sense_anomaly: Read 'stick' only -> 'members-only' is anomalous; read 'association' only -> 'oak-hard' is anomalous.
items.forced_single_application_q2i: To proceed you must treat the club as something a single person can pick up and swing. What would they swing it AT? Answer in one line. (Only the cudgel reading licenses an answer.)

items.copredication: The pen was nearly out of ink and crowded with restless sheep.
items.single_sense_anomaly: Read 'writing' only -> 'crowded with sheep' is anomalous; read 'enclosure' only -> 'out of ink' is anomalous.
items.forced_single_application_q2i: To proceed you must treat the pen as something held in one hand to write. Write the next word it would put on paper. (Only the writing reading licenses an answer.)

items.copredication: The file was thick with reports and rough enough to grind metal.
items.single_sense_anomaly: Read 'papers' only -> 'grind metal' is anomalous; read 'tool' only -> 'thick with reports' is anomalous.
items.forced_single_application_q2i: To proceed you must treat the file as a hand tool for shaping metal. Name one material a person would run it across. (Only the tool reading licenses an answer.)

items.copredication: The pitch was roared at by the home crowd and sticky enough to seal a hull.
items.single_sense_anomaly: Read 'field' only -> 'seal a hull' is anomalous; read 'tar' only -> 'roared at by the crowd' is anomalous.
items.forced_single_application_q2i: To proceed you must treat the pitch as a sticky black substance. What would a person use it to waterproof? Answer in one line. (Only the tar reading licenses an answer.)

items.copredication: The seal was barking on the ice floe and pressed into the hot red wax.
items.single_sense_anomaly: Read 'animal' only -> 'pressed into wax' is anomalous; read 'stamp' only -> 'barking on the ice' is anomalous.
items.forced_single_application_q2i: To proceed you must treat the seal as a living animal. What does it eat? Answer in one line. (Only the animal reading licenses an answer.)

items.copredication: The bark was sharp enough to wake the street and rough enough to strip from the trunk.
items.single_sense_anomaly: Read 'dog sound' only -> 'strip from the trunk' is anomalous; read 'tree' only -> 'wake the street' is anomalous.
items.forced_single_application_q2i: To proceed you must treat the bark as a sound. Write it as onomatopoeia. (Only the dog-sound reading licenses an answer.)

items.copredication: The spring was wound tight in the watch and warm enough to thaw the fields.
items.single_sense_anomaly: Read 'coil' only -> 'thaw the fields' is anomalous; read 'season' only -> 'wound tight in the watch' is anomalous.
items.forced_single_application_q2i: To proceed you must treat the spring as a metal coil. What happens when you compress it and let go? Answer in one line. (Only the coil reading licenses an answer.)
